headline: DOWNGRADE proposal. Material in-window event: April 27-28 2026 Google signed classified Pentagon AI deal allowing Gemini for any-lawful-government-purpose on classified networks despite 580+ employee letter (incl. 100+ DeepMind researchers, 20+ directors/VPs signed openly). Senior DeepMind scientist publicly stated 'incredibly ashamed.' This compounds February 2025 removal of weapons/surveillance pledge from AI Principles — a 14-month integrity-under-pressure trajectory. Counter-signal: same week Google quietly exited a $100M drone swarm contest (partial offset, not neutralizing). Composite -6.6, BND -15 driving the move; Established → Functional band crossing.
key_evidence: April 27-28 2026: Google signed classified Pentagon AI deal allowing Gemini on classified networks for 'any lawful government purpose' (mission planning, weapons targeting, etc.)
580+ Google employees signed open letter to CEO Pichai opposing deal; 100+ DeepMind researchers signed separate internal letter; 20+ directors/VPs signed openly
Senior DeepMind research scientist publicly stated 'incredibly ashamed'
February 2025: Google removed pledges from AI Principles to avoid building weapons or mass-surveillance technologies, with Hassabis citing 'global competition for AI leadership'
Anthropic's prior Pentagon exclusion was the precedent that opened this contract opportunity for Google
Counter-signal (same week): Google quietly exited a $100M drone swarm contest

evidence.finding: 580+ employees signed letter to CEO Pichai. Letter warns of 'irreparable damage to Google's reputation, business, and role in the world.' 20+ directors and VPs signed openly.
Google signed classified Pentagon AI deal despite documented employee opposition.
DeepMind scientists revolt; senior researcher publicly 'incredibly ashamed'.
Inhumane classified military AI use described in employee letter.
Workers urge Pichai reject classified work.
580+ Google employees including DeepMind researchers urge Pichai to refuse classified Pentagon AI deal.
Counter-signal: Google quietly exited a $100M drone swarm contest in the same week.

negativeSignals: Trajectory: Project Maven (2018) → AI Principles published (2018) → Hassabis 'global competition' framing + weapons-pledge removal (Feb 2025) → classified Pentagon deal signed (April 2026). 14-month integrity-under-pressure pattern. Internal opposition strongest of any AI lab in the cycle (580+ employees, 20+ directors/VPs publicly).
positiveSignals: Same-week $100M drone swarm contest exit (partial offset). Substantive technical safety work continues. Project Glasswing-type voluntary classification has not yet emerged at Google, but Frontier Model Forum participation and academic partnerships persist.
